description:
## Problem
`vitest.config.ts` has `passWithNoTests: true`. This setting allows the test runner to exit with success code 0 even if zero test files are found. If the `include: ['tests/**/*.test.ts']` glob somehow stops matching, or tests are deleted, CI will silently pass.

This is a CI safety net anti-pattern — it defeats the purpose of required test coverage.

## Fix
Remove `passWithNoTests: true` from `vitest.config.ts`. Once there are real tests in place (which there are — 131 passing), this option provides no value and hides potential regressions.

Also add `--coverage` to the test script OR create a separate `test:coverage` script. The coverage thresholds configured in vitest.config.ts (`statements: 80, branches: 75, functions: 90, lines: 80`) are never enforced because the base `vitest run` command doesn't collect coverage.

Recommended scripts:
- `"test": "vitest run"` — fast, no coverage (for dev)
- `"test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage"` — for CI

## Acceptance Criteria
- `passWithNoTests` is removed from vitest.config.ts
- `test:coverage` script added to package.json
- CI workflow runs `test:coverage` (not just `test`) so coverage thresholds are enforced
- All existing 131 tests still pass
title: Remove passWithNoTests: true from vitest.config.ts

descriptionHistory.description:
## Problem
`vitest.config.ts` has `passWithNoTests: true`. This setting allows the test runner to exit with success code 0 even if zero test files are found. If the `include: ['tests/**/*.test.ts']` glob somehow stops matching, or tests are deleted, CI will silently pass.

This is a CI safety net anti-pattern — it defeats the purpose of required test coverage.

## Fix
Remove `passWithNoTests: true` from `vitest.config.ts`. Once there are real tests in place (which there are — 131 passing), this option provides no value and hides potential regressions.

Also add `--coverage` to the test script OR create a separate `test:coverage` script. The coverage thresholds configured in vitest.config.ts (`statements: 80, branches: 75, functions: 90, lines: 80`) are never enforced because the base `vitest run` command doesn't collect coverage.

Recommended scripts:
- `"test": "vitest run"` — fast, no coverage (for dev)
- `"test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage"` — for CI

## Acceptance Criteria
- `passWithNoTests` is removed from vitest.config.ts
- `test:coverage` script added to package.json
- CI workflow runs `test:coverage` (not just `test`) so coverage thresholds are enforced
- All existing 131 tests still pass
